sections.problem: A retailer needed daily demand forecasts for key SKUs to align inventory and staffing. Naive methods (e.g. last week same day) led to stockouts and excess stock.
sections.data: Historical daily demand for 24 months, with clear seasonality (weekly and monthly) and a slight trend. Missing values were filled with forward fill and short gaps interpolated.
sections.approach: I compared a seasonal naive baseline, ARIMA with seasonal differencing, and a simple tree model on lag and calendar features. Validation was time-based: train on first 18 months, test on last 6. Metric: RMSE and MAPE.
sections.results: ARIMA(2,1,1)(1,1,1)[7] beat the naive baseline by 12% RMSE. The tree model was close but slightly worse on the tail of the horizon. The plot shows actual vs forecast for the test period.
next: Extend to multi-SKU with hierarchical reconciliation.
Pipe forecasts into an inventory optimization layer.

forecast.title: Actual vs forecast (test period, last 60 days)
